vulnerability.title: Prevent Login to Accounts With Empty Password
vulnerability.description: If an account is configured for password authentication but does not have an assigned password, it may be possible to log into the account without authentication. Remove any instances of the nullok in /etc/pam.d/system-auth and /etc/pam.d/password-auth to prevent logins with empty passwords.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then if [ -f /usr/bin/authselect ]; then if ! authselect check; then echo " authselect integrity check failed. Remediation aborted! This remediation could not be applied because an authselect profile was not selected or the selected profile is not intact. It is not recommended to manually edit the PAM files when authselect tool is available. In cases where the default authselect profile does not cover a specific demand, a custom authselect profile is recommended." exit 1 fi authselect enable-feature without-nullok authselect apply-changes -b else if grep -qP "^\s*auth\s+sufficient\s+pam_unix.so\s.*\bnullok\b" "/etc/pam.d/system-auth"; then sed -i -E --follow-symlinks "s/(.*auth.*sufficient.*pam_unix.so.*)\
vulnerability.group: Verify Proper Storage and Existence of Password Hashes

triage_decision.reason: This finding involves a standard STIG hardening pattern for PAM configurations to prevent empty password logins. The remediation script uses authselect or sed, which are safe and conventional methods for editing PAM files, aligning with the policy for safe_to_remediate for auth/ssh/sudo/pam changes.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential account lockout if accounts with empty passwords exist; May affect legacy applications relying on empty password authentication

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The finding is about preventing login to accounts with empty passwords. The rule is "no_empty_passwords" and the recommendation is to remove instances of "nullok" from /etc/pam.d/system-auth and /etc/pam.d/password-auth.

Let me first understand the issue:
- The nullok parameter in PAM modules allows users to log in with empty passwords.
- We need to remove any instances of "nullok" from the PAM configuration files.

I should:
1. First, inspect the current PAM configuration files to see if they contain "nullok"
2. Remove "nullok" from the appropriate lines, specifically from pam_unix.so modules

Let me start by reading the files to see their current state.
reasoning_messages.content: I'll help you remediate the OpenSCAP finding for preventing login to accounts with empty passwords. Let me start by examining the PAM configuration files to see the current state.
